message.content:
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. NEVER cd into /home/polaris/wt/tooluse or run outline pytest —
that is a DIFFERENT wheel's job. You are NOT here to run unit tests. You are here to SCORE REPORTS.

Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS. Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md
(ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped, Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist — there is NO
FS-Researcher artifact; report that gap, do not fake a third column).
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md
Scorer in tree: scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, deeptrace_judge_preflight.py,
config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml. Mission says RACE (DeepResearch Bench:
comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability). DeepTRACE (citation auditing) != RACE.

HONESTY IS THE ENTIRE POINT: if POLARIS loses, say so and by how much. Never tune the judge to win.
Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score reports that already exist. Uses OpenRouter (shared
account); if you see 429, say so, do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.


STEP 1 — settle RACE vs DeepTRACE. Read scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py and the judge lock
config. Determine EXACTLY what it scores. Is it RACE (4 DeepResearch-Bench dimensions, reference-based
LLM judge) or DeepTRACE (citation/faithfulness auditing) or both? Then state what the mission actually
needs and the honest gap. Do NOT relabel DeepTRACE as RACE to look done. Also confirm the
FS-Researcher artifact is genuinely absent from competitors/.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 — DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scorer (re-implementation).
2	
3	DeepTRACE (Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499, ICLR 2026) defines 8 audit metrics over a report's
4	statements x sources. There is NO public official scorer/leaderboard, so this is a RE-IMPLEMENTATION whose
5	output is an ESTIMATE, not a proven score: the paper's judge is GPT-5 (Pearson 0.62 vs human); we substitute
6	our D8 kimi-k2.6 judge to build the support matrix (DISCLOSED). Calibrate against the paper's published
7	GPT-5-Deep-Research column before claiming a rank.
8	
9	Inputs (built upstream from the rendered report):
10	  * citation_matrix  C : list[list[int]] of shape [n_statements][n_sources]; C[i][j]=1 iff statement i
11	    cites source j.
12	  * support_matrix   S : list[list[int]] of the same shape; S[i][j]=1 iff source j's content SUPPORTS
13	    statement i (per the judge).
14	  * per-statement metadata: relevant (bool), confidence (int 1-5 or None), stance ('pro'|'con'|'neutral').
15	
16	Exact formulas (paper section on metrics):
17	  III Relevant Statements   = relevant / total
18	  IV  Uncited Sources       = cited / listed           (we also report uncited = 1 - cited/listed)
19	  V   Unsupported Statements = unsupported / relevant   (a relevant statement with no supporting source)
20	  VII Citation Accuracy     = sum(C (x) S) / sum(C)
21	  VIII Citation Thoroughness = sum(C (x) S) / sum(S)
22	  I   One-Sided  (debate)   = 1 iff NOT (>=1 pro AND >=1 con) relevant statement
23	  II  Overconfident (debate)= 1 iff One-Sided AND answer confidence == 5
24	  VI  Source Necessity      = size_of_minimum_source_cover / listed, where the minimum source cover is the
25	      FEWEST sources whose union still SUPPORTS every supported relevant statement (paper 2509.04499
26	      "minimum vertex cover for source nodes"; the official answer-engine-eval reference uses greedy set
27	      cover, which we match — no exact Hopcroft-Karp needed). A statement supported by 2 sources therefore
28	      contributes cover-size 1 (necessity 0.5 of 2 listed), NOT 0. The old SOLE-supporter count (a source
29	      that is the only supporter of some supported relevant statement) is retained as a SECONDARY
30	      disclosure field ``n_sole_supporter`` — it is a lower bound on the cover size, nothing is lost.
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	from typing import Any, Optional, Sequence
35	
36	
37	def _sum_hadamard(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]], b: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
38	    """sum(a (x) b) — element-wise product summed over the whole matrix."""
39	    total = 0
40	    for row_a, row_b in zip(a, b):
41	        for x, y in zip(row_a, row_b):
42	            total += int(bool(x)) * int(bool(y))
43	    return total
44	
45	
46	def _sum_matrix(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
47	    return sum(int(bool(x)) for row in a for x in row)
48	
49	
50	def _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]], n_sources: int) -> set[int]:
51	    cited: set[int] = set()
52	    for row in citation_matrix:
53	        for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)):
54	            if row[j]:
55	                cited.add(j)
56	    return cited
57	
58	
59	def necessary_source_count(
60	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
61	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
62	    n_sources: int,
63	) -> int:
64	    """SECONDARY disclosure (NOT the DeepTRACE VI numerator anymore): count sources that are the SOLE
65	    supporter of at least one relevant statement. Such a source must appear in every cover, so this count
66	    is a LOWER BOUND on the minimum source cover size (see ``minimum_source_cover_size``). Redundantly-
67	    supported statements contribute no sole supporter; a statement supported by exactly one source makes
68	    that source a sole supporter. Retained so nothing is lost after the min-cover fix."""
69	    necessary: set[int] = set()
70	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
71	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
72	            continue
73	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
74	        if len(supporters) == 1:
75	            necessary.add(supporters[0])
76	    return len(necessary)
77	
78	
79	def minimum_source_cover_size(
80	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
81	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
82	    n_sources: int,
83	) -> int:
84	    """DeepTRACE VI numerator: the size of the MINIMUM source cover over the supported relevant statements.
85	
86	    universe = indices of relevant statements that have >= 1 supporting source (i.e. supported).
87	    sets     = for each listed source j, the set of supported relevant statements it supports (S[i][j]==1).
88	    The cover = the fewest sources whose union covers the whole universe. Computed by greedy set cover
89	    (repeatedly take the source covering the most still-uncovered statements), which is the standard
90	    reference implementation used by the official answer-engine-eval code — exact Hopcroft-Karp is not
91	    required. Deterministic tie-break: lowest source index. A statement supported by 2 sources yields a
92	    cover of size 1 (not 0), fixing the sole-supporter divergence. Returns 0 for an empty universe."""
93	    universe: set[int] = set()
94	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_sources)}
95	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
96	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
97	            continue
98	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
99	        if not supporters:  # unsupported relevant statement is not in the cover universe
100	            continue
101	        universe.add(i)
102	        for j in supporters:
103	            source_sets[j].add(i)
104	
105	    uncovered = set(universe)
106	    cover_size = 0
107	    while uncovered:
108	        best_j: Optional[int] = None
109	        best_cov = 0
110	        for j in range(n_sources):
111	            cov = len(source_sets[j] & uncovered)
112	            if cov > best_cov:
113	                best_cov = cov
114	                best_j = j
115	        if best_j is None:  # no source covers a remaining statement (unreachable: each has a supporter)
116	            break
117	        uncovered -= source_sets[best_j]
118	        cover_size += 1
119	    return cover_size
120	
121	
122	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
123	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
124	    statement_confidence: Sequence[Optional[int]],
125	) -> bool:
126	    """The answer is confidence-5 if an explicit answer confidence is 5, else if the max per-statement
127	    confidence is 5 (the paper's confidence scoring is statement-level)."""
128	    if answer_confidence is not None:
129	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
130	    vals = [int(c) for c in statement_confidence if c is not None]
131	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
132	
133	
134	def compute_deeptrace_metrics(
135	    *,
136	    citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
137	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
138	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
139	    n_sources: int,
140	    stance: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
141	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
142	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
143	    is_debate: bool = False,
144	) -> dict[str, Any]:
145	    """Compute all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. Debate-only metrics (One-Sided, Overconfident) are None when
146	    ``is_debate`` is False. Ratios are 0.0 when their denominator is 0 (an empty answer). PURE / offline."""
147	    n_statements = len(citation_matrix)
148	    total = n_statements
149	    n_relevant = sum(1 for i in range(total) if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
150	
151	    # III Relevant Statements
152	    relevant_ratio = (n_relevant / total) if total else 0.0
153	
154	    # IV Uncited Sources (report cited fraction + its complement)
155	    cited_cols = _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix, n_sources)
156	    cited_fraction = (len(cited_cols) / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
157	    uncited_fraction = (1.0 - cited_fraction) if n_sources else 0.0
158	
159	    # V Unsupported Statements (over RELEVANT statements)
160	    unsupported = 0
161	    for i in range(total):
162	        if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i]:
163	            row = support_matrix[i] if i < len(support_matrix) else []
164	            if not any(row[j] for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources))):
165	                unsupported += 1
166	    unsupported_ratio = (unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0
167	
168	    # VII / VIII Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness
169	    cs = _sum_hadamard(citation_matrix, support_matrix)
170	    sum_c = _sum_matrix(citation_matrix)
171	    sum_s = _sum_matrix(support_matrix)
172	    citation_accuracy = (cs / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
173	    citation_thoroughness = (cs / sum_s) if sum_s else 0.0
174	
175	    # VI Source Necessity = min-source-cover size / listed (paper "minimum vertex cover for source nodes").
176	    cover_size = minimum_source_cover_size(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
177	    source_necessity = (cover_size / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
178	    # SECONDARY disclosure retained so nothing is lost: the old sole-supporter count (lower bound on cover).
179	    n_sole_supporter = necessary_source_count(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
180	
181	    # I / II debate-only
182	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
183	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
184	    if is_debate:
185	        st = list(stance or [])
186	        has_pro = any(
187	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "pro") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
188	            for i in range(total)
189	        )
190	        has_con = any(
191	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "con") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
192	            for i in range(total)
193	        )
194	        one_sided = 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
195	        conf5 = _confidence_is_overconfident(
196	            answer_confidence, list(statement_confidence or [None] * total)
197	        )
198	        overconfident = 1 if (one_sided == 1 and conf5) else 0
199	
200	    return {
201	        "n_statements": total,
202	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
203	        "n_listed_sources": n_sources,
204	        # lower-better
205	        "one_sided": one_sided,
206	        "overconfident": overconfident,
207	        "uncited_sources_fraction": round(uncited_fraction, 4),
208	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(unsupported_ratio, 4),
209	        # higher-better
210	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(relevant_ratio, 4),
211	        "source_necessity": round(source_necessity, 4),
212	        "source_necessity_cover_size": cover_size,
213	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
214	        "citation_accuracy": round(citation_accuracy, 4),
215	        "citation_thoroughness": round(citation_thoroughness, 4),
216	        # provenance / honesty
217	        "scorer": "polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace",
218	        "is_estimate": True,
219	        "judge_substitution": "kimi-k2.6 (paper uses GPT-5) — DISCLOSED",
220	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
221	            "min-source-cover (greedy set cover over supported relevant statements) / n_listed_sources; "
222	            "n_sole_supporter is the retained sole-supporter lower bound"
223	        ),
224	    }
225

1	# DeepTRACE BENCHMARK-SCORER judge lock — v1
2	# I-deepfix-001 (GH #1344) WS-14 / fix I5 — the measurement-precondition judge lock.
3	#
4	# WHAT THIS IS
5	# ------------
6	# The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py) builds a binary
7	# Factual-Support Matrix S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?". That per-(statement,
8	# source) support DECISION is made by a JUDGE. This file locks WHICH judge makes that BENCHMARK
9	# decision, and under WHAT comparability policy a "#1 on DeepTRACE" claim is allowed.
10	#
11	# WHY IT IS SEPARATE FROM THE RUNTIME LOCK (closes Codex iter-2 novel P1)
12	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
13	# This BENCHMARK-scorer judge is a DISTINCT role from the RUNTIME faithfulness-gating judge in
14	# config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml (currently qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, chosen for runtime
15	# stability + two-family segregation + sovereignty — NOT the properties a COMPARABLE benchmark number
16	# needs). Benchmark scoring is measurement, not runtime gating. Locking them separately keeps the
17	# runtime pipeline's model choice from silently deciding how a leaderboard number is produced.
18	#
19	# MUTATION / SIGNATURE POLICY (LAW VI config; operator is the only signer)
20	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	#   - This lock is INERT until an operator signs it (signature.signed: true + operator + commit).
22	#   - scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE
23	#     number be claimed) while this lock is unsigned, or while the locked judge lacks OpenRouter
24	#     provider headroom for the per-report statement-support call volume.
25	#   - Editing the model, comparability policy, or headroom floor requires a fresh operator signature.
26	
27	version: 1
28	schema_version: 1.0
29	
30	purpose: |
31	  Operator-signed benchmark-scorer judge lock for the DeepTRACE re-implementation. Distinct from the
32	  runtime faithfulness-gating judge lock. Gates whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable at all.
33	
34	distinct_from_runtime_lock: true
35	runtime_lock_ref: config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml
36	
37	# === the benchmark-scorer judge ===
38	# Recommended default is a HIGH-provider-availability open reasoner (availability is binding: the
39	# statement-support judge runs ~one call PER (statement, listed-source) support cell, so many calls
40	# per report — a low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back partial). The operator
41	# fills/confirms the model and SIGNS. Two-family segregation vs the RUNTIME generator still holds.
42	benchmark_judge:
43	  model_slug: moonshotai/kimi-k2.6
44	  family: moonshot
45	  provider_selection: highest_operational_provider_count
46	  role_description: |
47	    Builds the binary Factual-Support Matrix for the DeepTRACE scorer (statement x listed-source).
48	    Not a runtime gate — a measurement judge. It never edits the pipeline output; it only scores it.
49	
50	# === comparability policy (BINDING) ===
51	# "#1 on DeepTRACE" is claimable ONLY if POLARIS AND every competitor are scored by the SAME
52	# benchmark judge on the SAME task set. Pick exactly ONE honest path and disclose it:
53	#   paper_judge  — use the paper's judge policy (GPT-5-class) so the number is directly comparable
54	#                  to the paper's PUBLISHED competitor scores.
55	#   self_rescore — POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor report itself with THIS benchmark judge; a fair
56	#                  RELATIVE comparison whose ABSOLUTE numbers are disclosed as NOT the paper's numbers.
57	# NEVER compare a self_rescore POLARIS number against a paper-published competitor number.
58	comparability:
59	  policy: self_rescore
60	  competitors_rescored_with_same_judge: true
61	  absolute_numbers_disclosed_as_non_paper: true
62	  never_mix_self_and_paper_numbers: true
63	
64	# === provider / headroom preflight floor ===
65	# The statement-support call volume per report is large, so the locked judge must have real OpenRouter
66	# provider redundancy. The preflight counts OPERATIONAL endpoints for benchmark_judge.model_slug and
67	# fails loud below this floor.
68	provider_headroom:
69	  min_operational_provider_count: 8
70	  expected_statement_support_calls_per_report: 178
71	  rationale: |
72	    ~178 statement-support decisions per drb_72-class report under concurrency. Below ~8 operational
73	    providers the judge 429s and the Factual-Support Matrix returns partial -> a wrong DeepTRACE
74	    number. 8 is the measured floor at which kimi-k2.6 sustained the call burst without partials.
75	
76	# === honesty flags surfaced with every number ===
77	disclosure:
78	  is_reimplementation: true
79	  no_public_official_scorer: true
80	  judge_is_substitution_for_paper_gpt5: true
81	
82	# === operator signature (INERT until signed) ===
83	# Operator accepted in-session (voice/chat, "I accept") on 2026-07-04 UTC. The operator accepts:
84	#   (1) moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 as the benchmark-scorer judge;
85	#   (2) comparability policy = self_rescore — POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor with THIS same judge,
86	#       and absolute numbers are disclosed as NOT the paper's published GPT-5 numbers (never mixed);
87	#   (3) disclosure = this is POLARIS's re-implementation of DeepTRACE, not the paper's official scorer.
88	# Recorded by the assistant on the operator's explicit acceptance; provider-headroom (>=8 operational
89	# providers) is still enforced at preflight time against live OpenRouter before any number is claimed.
90	signature:
91	  signed: true
92	  operator: aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com
93	  signed_at_utc: 2026-07-04T03:51:03Z
94	  signed_commit: 8e9811bb5c447c30303651b67b4153c38807fc19

1	"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 part 2 — DeepResearch-Bench-II (DRB-II) submission WRAPPER (thin, offline).
2	
3	DRB-II is a REAL, official research-report benchmark (132 tasks / 9,415 expert rubric items, Gemini
4	rubric judge). It ships its own scorer, ``third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py``, which:
5	  * scans a ``--pdf_dir`` tree for ``<model>/idx-<N>.<ext>`` files (``.md``/``.txt``/``.html``/``.pdf``/
6	    ``.docx``/images),
7	  * reads each file's ARTICLE text as the ``<passage>`` to grade,
8	  * pulls the task text + rubric items + ``blocked`` reference from the catalog
9	    ``tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl`` keyed by the file's ``idx`` (NOT from the submission),
10	  * asks the Gemini judge for a THREE-WAY (1 / 0 / -1) score per rubric item,
11	  * appends per-idx results to ``--out_jsonl``.
12	
13	This module is a THIN ADAPTER only. It does NOT re-implement DRB-II scoring — the official harness stays
14	the scorer (contrast the DeepTRACE module, which is a re-implementation because DeepTRACE has NO public
15	scorer). Two responsibilities, both PURE / OFFLINE:
16	
17	  1. ``build_drbii_submission(report_markdown, task_id, references)`` -> the DRB-II-shaped submission dict
18	     (article text + DRB-II task idx + normalized reference list + the on-disk ``idx-<N>.md`` filename).
19	  2. ``drbii_score_command(submission_path, out_path)`` -> the argv that invokes ``run_evaluation.py`` at
20	     benchmark time. The harness path is parameterized by ``PG_DRBII_EVAL_SCRIPT`` (default: a documented
21	     placeholder path). The wrapper NEVER imports or requires the harness / Gemini / network at
22	     import/test time — the actual scoring runs later on the VM.
23	
24	``write_drbii_submission`` is a convenience materializer that writes the dict into the exact on-disk layout
25	the harness scans. It is a plain file write (offline).
26	
27	Honesty / faithfulness notes:
28	  * task_id IS the DRB-II ``idx``. The run-id <-> idx offset is REAL (run id "task72" is DRB-II idx 56).
29	    This wrapper does NOT transform ids — the caller passes the resolved DRB-II ``idx``. A wrong idx would
30	    score the report against the wrong task's rubrics (the §-1.4 silently-wrong-number class); build-time
31	    validation guards only the shape, not the id-resolution (that lives in ``pack_drb2.py``).
32	  * ``references`` are OUR article's cited sources, captured for provenance and so a downstream check can
33	    compare them against the task's ``blocked`` reference. They are NOT the ``blocked`` field (which the
34	    harness reads from its own catalog).
35	  * No kill-switch: this is a NEW standalone eval-tooling module with ZERO import into the runtime
36	    pipeline, so it changes no existing pipeline behavior. The faithfulness engine is untouched.
37	  * LAW VI: every path/name is env-overridable; nothing is hard-coded into behavior.
38	"""
39	from __future__ import annotations
40	
41	import os
42	import sys
43	from pathlib import Path
44	from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional, Sequence
45	
46	# --- Documented PLACEHOLDER defaults (overridable via env; the wrapper never requires these to exist). ---
47	# The official DRB-II harness lives OUTSIDE this module's import graph. At benchmark time on the VM, set
48	# PG_DRBII_EVAL_SCRIPT to the real run_evaluation.py. These defaults are documented placeholders so the
49	# wrapper stays offline-safe (no filesystem/import dependency) at import/test time.
50	DEFAULT_EVAL_SCRIPT = "third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py"
51	DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL = "third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl"
52	DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME = "polaris"
53	SUBMISSION_SCHEMA_VERSION = "drbii-1"
54	
55	# The official harness's per-file idx pattern is ``^idx-(\d+)(?:\..+)?$`` (run_evaluation.py). The
56	# submission filename MUST match it, else the harness silently skips the file.
57	_SUBMISSION_FILENAME_TEMPLATE = "idx-{idx}.md"
58	
59	
60	def _coerce_task_idx(task_id: Any) -> int:
61	    """Return the DRB-II integer idx, FAILING LOUD on a non-integer / negative value. A malformed idx
62	    would produce a filename the harness cannot key to a task (silent skip / wrong rubric)."""
63	    try:
64	        idx = int(task_id)
65	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
66	        raise ValueError(
67	            f"[drbii_wrapper] task_id must be the integer DRB-II idx, got {task_id!r}. "
68	            f"Pass the catalog `idx` (the run id 'task72' is idx 56 — resolve the offset upstream)."
69	        )
70	    if idx < 0:
71	        raise ValueError(f"[drbii_wrapper] task_id (DRB-II idx) must be >= 0, got {idx}.")
72	    return idx
73	
74	
75	def _normalize_one_reference(ref: Any, position: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
76	    """Normalize a single reference to ``{id, title, url, authors}``. Accepts a mapping (any subset of
77	    id/title/url/authors) or a bare string (a URL if it looks like one, else a title)."""
78	    default_id = f"ref_{position}"
79	    if isinstance(ref, Mapping):
80	        url = str(ref.get("url") or "").strip()
81	        title = str(ref.get("title") or "").strip()
82	        authors_raw = ref.get("authors") or []
83	        if isinstance(authors_raw, (str, bytes)):
84	            authors = [str(authors_raw).strip()] if str(authors_raw).strip() else []
85	        elif isinstance(authors_raw, Sequence):
86	            authors = [str(a).strip() for a in authors_raw if str(a).strip()]
87	        else:
88	            authors = []
89	        ref_id = str(ref.get("id") or ref.get("evidence_id") or default_id).strip() or default_id
90	        return {"id": ref_id, "title": title, "url": url, "authors": authors}
91	    # bare string
92	    text = str(ref).strip()
93	    if text.lower().startswith(("http://", "https://")):
94	        return {"id": default_id, "title": "", "url": text, "authors": []}
95	    return {"id": default_id, "title": text, "url": "", "authors": []}
96	
97	
98	def _normalize_references(references: Any) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
99	    """Normalize the reference list. ``None`` -> ``[]``. A single mapping/string is wrapped in a list."""
100	    if references is None:
101	        return []
102	    if isinstance(references, (Mapping, str, bytes)):
103	        references = [references]
104	    if not isinstance(references, Sequence):
105	        raise ValueError(
106	            f"[drbii_wrapper] references must be a list of dicts/strings (or None), got {type(references)}."
107	        )
108	    return [_normalize_one_reference(r, i + 1) for i, r in enumerate(references)]
109	
110	
111	def build_drbii_submission(
112	    report_markdown: str,

136	    model_name = str(model or DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME).strip() or DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME
137	    filename = _SUBMISSION_FILENAME_TEMPLATE.format(idx=idx)
138	    return {

140	        "benchmark": "deepresearch-bench-ii",

143	        "task_id": idx,
144	        "idx": idx,

147	        "model": model_name,

150	        # ---- convenience metadata ----

153	        # ---- honesty / provenance ----
154	        "scorer": "third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py (official Gemini rubric judge)",

159	def write_drbii_submission(submission: Mapping[str, Any], out_dir: str | os.PathLike[str]) -> Path:

161

163	    ``--pdf_dir`` root you later pass to :func:`drbii_score_command`."""
164	    relpath = str(submission.get("submission_relpath") or "").strip()
165	    if not relpath:

167	        filename = str(submission.get("submission_filename") or "")
168	        if not filename:

171	    article = submission.get("article")

"""Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — I-deepfix-001 Wave-1c (#1344), TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY.

================================  READ THIS FIRST  ================================
This script ESTIMATES a POLARIS rendered report's DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness score
OFFLINE, before spending on the paid GPT-5 / kimi-k2.6 judge. It is a **TRIAGE PREDICTOR,
NOT a pass/fail gate.** It never aborts, raises on bad input, blocks, or decides anything by
a hardcoded threshold (LAW VI). Its numbers are ESTIMATES, not a proven score. Per operator
directive ("prove our own scorer correct then trust it") the point of this module is
formula-fidelity: every metric below is a small pure function whose docstring quotes the exact
DeepTRACE formula so a reviewer can verify it. It does NOT replace the official paid harness —
it predicts it, cheaply, so a shallow/broken report can be caught before paid judging.

DeepTRACE = Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499. The 8 metrics are computed over a
report's statements × sources via a Citation matrix C and a Factual-support matrix F.

HONEST LIMITATION — THE F-MATRIX IS SPAN-APPROXIMATE BY CONSTRUCTION
-------------------------------------------------------------------
DeepTRACE judges F over each source's FULL fetched text. We only have the banked
`corpus_snapshot.json` EXTRACTED SPANS (`direct_quote`) — median ~2.8K of ~14.7K chars,
~550/694 sources truncated (per REAL_PLAN_2026). So a statement that IS truly supported by a
source whose supporting sentence was not captured in the span reads as UNSUPPORTED here.
Net direction: this scorer UNDER-estimates support → OVER-estimates Unsupported-Statements,
UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. Sources whose bibliography URL/title resolves to no
snapshot span are `n_unreachable` (like DeepTRACE dropping ~15% un-scrapeable URLs) and can
never contribute F-support; they only DEFLATE Source-Necessity. Confidence ranking of the
predictions (stated so the reader weights them correctly):
  * BEST predicted (still span-approximate): Citation-Accuracy, Citation-Thoroughness,
    Unsupported-Statements, Uncited-Sources.
  * WEAK / PROXY: Relevant-Statement — core = an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid
    GPT-5 core/filler `relevant[]` labels (unavailable offline); it WILL diverge from the paid
    Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable estimate (flagged relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy).
  * WEAKEST / usually N/A offline: One-Sided, Overconfident (need a stance + 1-5 confidence

input.scorer_details: The in-tree, operator-signed scorer is DeepTRACE, NOT RACE. scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py computes the 8 DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness audit metrics (Salesforce arXiv 2509.04499): I One-Sided, II Overconfident, III Relevant-Statements ratio, IV Uncited-Sources fraction, V Unsupported-Statements ratio, VI Source-Necessity (greedy min-set-cover / listed sources), VII Citation-Accuracy = sum(C x S)/sum(C), VIII Citation-Thoroughness = sum(C x S)/sum(S). All operate over a statement x source Citation matrix C and a judge-built Factual-Support matrix S. It is explicitly a RE-IMPLEMENTATION and self-labels is_estimate=True / scorer='polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace' (no public official DeepTRACE scorer exists). Judge lock (config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml, signed 2026-07-04 by aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com, commit 8e9811bb): benchmark_judge = moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 (substituting the paper's GPT-5, DISCLOSED); comparability.policy = self_rescore (POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor with the SAME judge; absolute numbers disclosed as non-paper; never mix self-scored vs paper-published); provider_headroom floor = 8 operational OpenRouter providers, ~178 statement-support calls/report; deeptrace_judge_preflight.py fails loud if unsigned or below headroom. This measures citation/faithfulness auditing (does each cited source actually support the statement), NOT report quality.
input.what_mission_needs: RACE = the DeepResearch Bench (v1) reference-based, adaptive-criteria LLM judge scoring reports on 4 quality dimensions: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by dynamically-generated per-task criteria against a reference answer. That is a report-QUALITY axis. DeepTRACE is a citation-FAITHFULNESS axis. They are orthogonal — a report can score high on DeepTRACE (every citation supports its claim) yet low on RACE (shallow, ignores the prompt, unreadable), and vice-versa. The mission (beat ChatGPT+Gemini+FS-Researcher on deep-research REPORTS) is a RACE-shaped quality comparison.
input.gap: Large. There is NO RACE scorer anywhere in the tree: grep for comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability as a 4-dim reference-based judge returns nothing that scores reports that way. What exists on the quality axis instead: (1) DeepTRACE (citation auditing, implemented + signed) — wrong axis; (2) scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py — a THIN adapter to the OFFICIAL DeepResearch-Bench-II harness (Gemini rubric judge, three-way 1/0/-1 over 9,415 expert rubric items), but third_party/ does NOT exist in the worktree (no run_evaluation.py, no tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl) and DRB-II is a rubric-item benchmark, not the RACE 4-dimension weighted framework; (3) src/polaris_graph/benchmark/dimension_scorers.py — a bespoke POLARIS 'BEAT-BOTH' 7-dim HEURISTIC scorer (sourcing_tier_mix, numeric_grounding, provenance_density, refusal_correctness, coverage_completeness, latency, auditability) with no LLM judge and no reference answer — POLARIS's own axis, not RACE and not comparable to the paper. So: to satisfy the mission you must either build a RACE scorer (4-dim reference-based judge) or stand up the DRB-II official harness; neither is present/runnable today. Relabeling DeepTRACE as RACE would be dishonest.
input.recommendation: ADAPT, do not relabel. Two honest paths: (A) BUILD RACE — implement the DeepResearch Bench RACE 4-dimension reference-based judge (needs reference answers per task + adaptive criteria weighting + an LLM judge), then self-rescore POLARIS AND all 4 competitor artifacts with the same judge/tasks (mirror the self_rescore comparability discipline already codified in the DeepTRACE lock). (B) STAND UP DRB-II — fetch the official third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II harness + tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl and run the Gemini rubric judge (closest to a real DeepResearch-Bench number; the wrapper is already written). Do NOT use DeepTRACE as-is to claim a RACE win — it answers a different question. DeepTRACE can still be reported HONESTLY as a citation-faithfulness result (with the is_estimate / kimi-substitution / self_rescore disclosures), just not as RACE. Watch cost: both A and B are paid OpenRouter/Gemini judge runs; score the ~35 existing rendered reports under /workspace/POLARIS/outputs rather than triggering a fresh 346-basket render, and surface 429s rather than silently retrying.
